---
title: "R Episode 8 — Publication-Quality Graphics with ggplot2"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Objectives
By the end of this episode you will be able to:

- Build a plot with the grammar of graphics: data, aesthetics, geoms.
- Map variables to color, size, and facets.
- Add scales, labels, and themes for publication quality.

**Teaching:** 40 min · **Exercises:** 30 min
:::

## The grammar of graphics

Every ggplot has three core parts: **data**, **aesthetic mappings** (`aes()`), and one or more
**geoms** (layers). You add layers with `+`.

```r
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(data = gapminder, mapping = aes(x = gdpPercap, y = lifeExp)) +
  geom_point()
```

## Mapping more variables

```r
ggplot(gapminder, aes(x = gdpPercap, y = lifeExp, color = continent, size = pop)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_x_log10()                      # GDP is highly skewed
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Instructor note
Distinguish **mapping** an aesthetic (inside `aes()`, driven by data) from **setting** it (outside
`aes()`, a constant like `alpha = 0.6`). Putting a constant inside `aes()` is the #1 ggplot
confusion — it creates a spurious legend.
:::

## Facets: small multiples

```r
ggplot(gapminder, aes(x = year, y = lifeExp, group = country)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~ continent)
```

## Publication polish

```r
ggplot(gapminder, aes(x = gdpPercap, y = lifeExp, color = continent)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::dollar) +
  labs(
    title = "Wealth and life expectancy",
    x = "GDP per capita (log scale)",
    y = "Life expectancy (years)",
    color = "Continent"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```

Save with `ggsave()` at a defined size and resolution for reproducible figures:

```r
ggsave(here::here("output", "wealth-lifeexp.png"), width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Challenge — Trends over time
Make a line plot of `lifeExp` over `year` for the countries of Oceania, colored by country.

### Solution

```r
oceania <- gapminder[gapminder$continent == "Oceania", ]
ggplot(oceania, aes(x = year, y = lifeExp, color = country)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(y = "Life expectancy", color = "Country") +
  theme_minimal()
```
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Key points
- ggplot = data + `aes()` mappings + geom layers, combined with `+`.
- Map variables inside `aes()`; set constants outside it.
- `facet_wrap()` makes small multiples; `labs()`, `scale_*`, and `theme_*` give polish.
- `ggsave()` exports reproducible figures at a fixed size/DPI.
:::

Next: [Episode 9 — Vectorization](r-09-vectorization.qmd).
